title: Guidonia esculenta
taxonomy: Guidonia esculenta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This family includes over 1,800 species of plants commonly known as morning glories or bindweeds. Guidonia esculenta is native to tropical regions and is often grown for its edible tubers. It is a type of root vegetable that is commonly consumed in Southeast Asia. Guidonia esculenta is also known as the 'taro bean' or 'edible morning glory'.
uses: Guidonia esculenta, also known as the taro plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and tubers are rich in vitamins and minerals, and have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The root of the plant is also used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant's leaves have been used to treat wounds and reduce inflammation. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds.
